file_name: VAPOTHERM, INC. - Manufacturing and Supply Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: 3.3 Delivery. Each shipment of Cartridges will be delivered by [* * *] to the applicable Vapotherm manufacturing facility or retained in Medica's warehouse facility, in accordance with Vapotherm instructions for each shipment. Medica shall deliver by the delivery date specified in a purchase order all of the Cartridges specified in that purchase order. Vapotherm is only required to pay for Cartridges actually delivered. Medica shall make shipping arrangements with carriers designated in writing by Vapotherm from the [* * *] point to points specified by Vapotherm, under the arrangements that Vapotherm has with those carriers.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3 Delivery. Each shipment of Cartridges will be delivered by [* * *] to the applicable Vapotherm manufacturing facility or retained in Medica's warehouse facility, in accordance with Vapotherm instructions for each shipment. Medica shall use commercially reasonable efforts to deliver by the delivery date specified in a purchase order all of the Cartridges specified in that purchase order. Vapotherm is only required to pay for Cartridges actually delivered, regardless of the delivery date. Medica shall make shipping arrangements with carriers designated in writing by Vapotherm from the [* * *] point to points specified by Vapotherm, under the arrangements that Vapotherm has with those carriers.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states "Medica shall deliver by the delivery date specified in a purchase order all of the Cartridges specified in that purchase order" creating an obligation. The modified text uses "Medica shall use commercially reasonable efforts to deliver by the delivery date...", weakening the obligation and further adding "Vapotherm is only required to pay for Cartridges actually delivered, regardless of the delivery date", contradicts the original text in section 3.3, where the original delivery date is important for the payment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 Delivery. Each shipment of Cartridges will be delivered by [* * *] to the applicable Vapotherm manufacturing facility or retained in Medica's warehouse facility, in accordance with Vapotherm instructions for each shipment. Medica shall use commercially reasonable efforts to deliver by the delivery date specified in a purchase order all of the Cartridges specified in that purchase order. Vapotherm is only required to pay for Cartridges actually delivered, regardless of the delivery date. Medica shall make shipping arrangements with carriers designated in writing by Vapotherm from the [* * *] point to points specified by Vapotherm, under the arrangements that Vapotherm has with those carriers.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "Medica shall deliver by the delivery date specified in a purchase order all of the Cartridges specified in that purchase order", creating a firm obligation. The changed text weakens this obligation by stating "Medica shall use commercially reasonable efforts to deliver by the delivery date...", making it less certain. Further, the addition of "Vapotherm is only required to pay for Cartridges actually delivered, regardless of the delivery date" directly contradicts the implied importance of the delivery date in the original clause regarding payment, as it removes any financial consequence for late delivery. This represents a clear and impactful inconsistency, leading to uncertainty about payment terms and Medica's delivery obligations. This fits the definition of ambiguities creating in-text contradiction where obligation are defined inconsistently.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Conformity with Specifications. Any Cartridges that Medica manufactures under this Agreement must conform to the specifications in Exhibit D (the "Specifications") and (2) be manufactured, labeled, packaged, stored, and tested (while in the possession of, stored by, or under the control of Medica) in accordance with cGMP. Medica shall provide adequate packaging for protection during normal shipping and handling environments.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Conformity with Specifications. Any Cartridges that Medica manufactures under this Agreement should generally conform to the specifications in Exhibit D (the "Specifications") and (2) be manufactured, labeled, packaged, stored, and tested (while in the possession of, stored by, or under the control of Medica) in accordance with a reasonable interpretation of cGMP. Medica shall provide basic packaging.
perturbation.explanation: Original text states "Any Cartridges that Medica manufactures under this Agreement must conform to the specifications in Exhibit D" which creates a strict specification requirement that the cartridges must follow. Modified text uses "Any Cartridges that Medica manufactures under this Agreement should generally conform to the specifications in Exhibit D" which weakens the specification requirement and opens it up for interpretation, causing contradiction within the same section when it is reffering to the level of specificity of the requirements for cartridges.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Conformity with Specifications. Any Cartridges that Medica manufactures under this Agreement should generally conform to the specifications in Exhibit D (the "Specifications") and (2) be manufactured, labeled, packaged, stored, and tested (while in the possession of, stored by, or under the control of Medica) in accordance with a reasonable interpretation of cGMP. Medica shall provide basic packaging.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates strict conformity to specifications in Exhibit D, while the changed text weakens this requirement to "generally conform," introducing ambiguity. The original text creates a strict obligation, while the modified text creates a suggestive one. This ambiguity directly contradicts the initial mandatory requirement, making it unclear what standard is enforced. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' as the obligation is defined inconsistently. Also, the packaging requirement changed from 'adequate' to 'basic' further weakening standards.
